intro: Decaloba penduliflora, also known as the Mexican prairie clover, is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Decaloba penduliflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
